hypothesis: Si–X silicon alloy anodes that exhibit a computed slope of Li-ion migration channel radius versus state of charge ≤0.45 Å per 100% lithiation (from CHGNet-relaxed delithiation pathways) will display <20% electrode thickness change and ≥85% capacity retention after 500 cycles at 0.5C in LiPF6/EC:DEC with 10% FEC, whereas alloys with slopes >0.45 Å per 100% will fail at least one of these benchmarks.
